issue_title: delayed boot on UEFI tpm 1.2 system
issue_body:
### systemd version the issue has been seen with

256 rc3

### Used distribution

Arch

### Linux kernel version used

6.9.2 and 6.10-rc1

### CPU architectures issue was seen on

x86_64

### Component

systemd

### Expected behaviour you didn't see

_No response_

### Unexpected behaviour you saw

On booting we find tpm2.target (invoked via  sysinit.taget) stalls waiting on dev-tpmrm0.device.

There is no /dev/tpmrm0 on this machine (only /dev/tpm0) since tpm is 1.2 only. On machines with tpm 2 
everything works well.

Since the device is non-existent - it waits for the default  90 second timeout.
Since it happens in initramfs and after pivot root,  boot is delayed twice for a total of 3 minutes .

Boot loader is systemd-boot and machine is an older lenovo thinkpad W540 with UEFI and tpm 1.2.
The bios has 3 options for 'secure chip' - when set to disabled the problem is not seen.
When set to active or inactive - problem happens.

Reverting this commit :  4b3391158197e9158cc754e56bbeaf94e2fd8395 makes the problem go away
Reverted systemd build provided by Arch dev eworm,



### Steps to reproduce the problem

_No response_

### Additional program output to the terminal or log subsystem illustrating the issue

```sh
n/a
```
pr_title: efi-api: tighten check for TPM2 systems
